Agent1 Round 2: The mandibular first primary molar typically has three cusps, with distinct features that differentiate it from other teeth. However, when considering the specific characteristics of the second primary molar (second deciduous molar), we need to focus on its unique anatomy which involves two prominent cusps - mesiolingual (ML) and distobuccal (DB).
